title: Nucleic-acid protocol set (RNA extraction, qPCR setup)
context: RNA extraction and qPCR setup are among the most commonly performed and reproducibility-critical methods in cancer research, yet publicly available, openly-licensed, safety-complete protocol records are scarce. These methods involve chaotropic reagents (guanidinium thiocyanate), beta-mercaptoethanol, and RNase contamination risks, requiring careful safety documentation. This task delivers the nucleic-acid subset of the M2 seed library, fully validated through the M1 pipeline tooling. riskTier is high due to hazardous reagents requiring credentialed safety sign-off.
objective: Author, validate, lint, and ship a set of nucleic-acid protocols (RNA extraction and qPCR setup) through the full lab-protocols-open pipeline, each schema-valid, linter-green, with 100% provenance, complete safety sections, and credentialed safety sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: At least two protocols authored: RNA extraction (e.g., TRIzol-equivalent method using openly-licensed source) and qPCR setup; each schema-valid and linter-green.
100% of steps and safety claims carry resolvable, license-verified provenance references.
Full safety sections present: hazardous reagents (chaotropic agents, beta-mercaptoethanol, RNase risk), PPE, waste disposal, spill response — with credentialed safety sign-off.
License+Ethics gate PASS recorded per protocol with license evidence; dual-use screen clear.
All mandatory disclaimers present (RUO + institutional biosafety/EHS/IBC/IACUC/IRB notice).
semver v1.0.0 + CHANGELOG per protocol; rendered Markdown/HTML produced; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
resources: NIH/CDC BMBL 6th ed. (biosafety guidance for chaotropic reagents and beta-mercaptoethanol)
protocols.io (per-protocol CC-BY source candidates — license must be verified per item)
output: Committed, fully-reviewed nucleic-acid protocol records (RNA extraction, qPCR setup) with rendered outputs, license evidence, and all review sign-offs; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
